extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint by a quarter wheel over Jakobsen after Molano's leadout acceleration was timed too early on the wide boulevard finish.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck hit the front too early on the wide 1.5km boulevard, allowing swarm from behind despite strong Molano leadout.
Alpecin-Fenix demonstrates pattern: patience pays on wide finishes, launching latest from left side while others fade right.
UAE's DE MAAR finishing 14th reveals major sprint train dysfunction, replicating Tour de France positioning failures despite flat tailored profile.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Third consecutive Grand Tour opening sprint stage win for Alpecin-Fenix shows repeatable late-acceleration pattern on long straights
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastian: neutral: Strong leadout acceleration insufficient to hold off Philipsen, suggests UAE lacks pure sprint finisher to convert positioning
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: Led sprint early but couldn't sustain power through full 1.5km straight, positioning advantage wasted
rider_signals.DE MAAR Arnaud: negative: 14th place finish after flat sprint stage mirrors Tour de France failures, indicates systemic UAE sprint train issues
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Couldn't match Molano's acceleration despite good position, suggesting early-season form still building
